idScheme: Identity is per label; there is no shared :Symbol base. A PHPClass id is its FQN verbatim; a PHPMethod id is `<owner FQN>::<method name>`. Kind lives in a secondary label and the `kind` property, never in the id. Event id is the event name; CronGroup id is the group id; Package ids are `package:<vendor>/<name>`; author ids are `author:<name>|<email>|<homepage>`.

nodeKinds.note: Enum cases are not modeled as nodes.

Magento di.xml <virtualType>: a named pre-configured variant of a base class. EXTENDS its base class and is referenced by name wherever a class FQN is expected — so it is a PHPClass (kind=virtualType), not a separate node family.

Referenced-only class-like anchor whose declaring file was outside the analyzed scope, so its specific kind is unknown (no secondary kind label). Also used for class FQNs referenced by Magento di.xml/events.xml that may not be declared in scope.

id is `<owner FQN>::<name>`. returnType/paramTypes hold scalar spellings only; class-like types are edges. paramTypes is index-aligned with paramNames. parametersJson is a JSON string of per-parameter optional/variadic/byRef/promoted flags.

A Magento event (dispatch hook) named in events.xml. id is the event name (lowercase_underscore). Target of OBSERVES edges from observer classes. Created on reference (events are fired implicitly in PHP, never declared).
A Magento cron group. id is the group id from crontab.xml/cron_groups.xml. Settings come from cron_groups.xml (all optional, scalar); SCHEDULED_IN edges from methods come from crontab.xml.
A Magento REST/SOAP endpoint URL from webapi.xml. id is the url (e.g. /V1/products/:sku). The HTTP verb lives on the SERVED_BY edge, so one URL node has one SERVED_BY edge per verb.

A Magento extension attribute (extra API field) from extension_attributes.xml. id is `<for FQN>::<code>`. Like a method: scalar type kept in the `type` property (empty when class-like); a class-like type is an OF_TYPE edge to the PHPClass.
composerNodeKind is `installed` (present in composer.lock) or `placeholder` (only referenced as a dependency target; carries just id/name/vendor/packageName/composerNodeKind).

relationshipTypes.note: Class extends class, interface extends interface, or a VirtualType extends its base class (the VirtualType case sets area/sourceFile, from di.xml).
Union types and per-position params each get their own edge (identity folds in position).
Built by index-links via longest-matching PSR-4 prefix against Package.psr4Namespaces. Methods inherit their owner's package. classmap/psr-0 symbols are not linked.

Magento di.xml <preference>: the requested type (interface/class) is wired to a concrete type. area is the Magento area (global|frontend|adminhtml|crontab|webapi_rest|webapi_soap|graphql|setup); the same `from` can have a distinct edge per area. Built by the index-xml pipeline.

Magento di.xml <plugin>: the plugin (interceptor) class targets another class. area as above. Which target method a plugin intercepts is not stored; it is derivable at query time from PLUGIN_FOR + HAS_METHOD using the before/after/around method-name convention.

Magento di.xml constructor <argument> wiring (composition), from the configured class/virtualType to the injected type. Distinct from PARAM_TYPE on purpose: PARAM_TYPE is the complete declared parameter list on the __construct method (often interfaces); INJECTS is the di-configured concrete subset (classes/virtualTypes, area-specific). Combine at query time by argument name. Scalar argument values (string/boolean/number/init_parameter/const) are not edges; each object item of an array argument gets its own edge with is_array=true.

Magento events.xml <observer>: the observer class listens to an event. area-specific (global/frontend/adminhtml/...). disabled=true means an inherited observer is switched off. The observer always implements ObserverInterface::execute (no per-event method in Magento 2).

Magento crontab.xml <job>: the method (instance + method) runs as a cron job in the group. jobName is the job's unique name; schedule is the cron expression (may be empty when the job uses a config_path instead). The job binding is the edge; the group node carries its settings from cron_groups.xml. When the cron instance is a virtualType or the method is inherited, the method node is an anchor — resolve the real class via split(id,'::') + EXTENDS*0.. + HAS_METHOD.

Magento webapi.xml <route><service>: the URL is served by a service class::method. The HTTP verb is on the edge (httpMethod folds into identity), so one URL has one edge per verb. webapi.xml is global, so no area. ACL <resources> is not captured.
Magento extension_attributes.xml: a data interface gains an extra API field (the ExtensionAttribute node). Global, no area.
The class-like type of an extension attribute (scalar types stay in the ExtensionAttribute.type property instead).
Platform requirements (php, ext-*, lib-*) are excluded.

typeMappingRules: Class/interface/enum/trait param and return types become PARAM_TYPE / RETURNS_TYPE edges; fundamental scalars (int, string, bool, float, array, void, null, ...) stay as returnType/paramTypes string fields and never become nodes.
Native signature types are edges with source=native; docblock @param/@return class types are added with source=docblock only when not already present natively at that position.
A docblock array element (Foo[], array<int,Foo>, list<Foo>, iterable<Foo>) becomes an edge with is_array=true; native `array` stays a scalar field.
An untyped param/return backfills a fundamental scalar from the docblock. Unresolvable, pseudo, or malformed docblock types (class-string, names with '-', unparsable text) are dropped entirely.

Implementations of an interface method are resolvable at query time via IMPLEMENTS/EXTENDS/USES + HAS_METHOD name match; no override edge is stored. The method-call graph and member nodes (properties, constants, enum cases) are out of scope.
